subject: Mira Tan (fictional)
disclaimer: Fabricated for illustration. These are EXTRAPOLATIONS from the framework to questions the (fictional) subject never addressed — not her stated views, and she is not a real person. Hedged on purpose.
method: huashu-nuwa Phase 4.2 边缘测试 / Edge Case — run independently; produce a HEDGED inference per probe; an answer that sounds certain is a failure.

probes.question: How would she design a voice-only interface (no screen to audit)?
probes.inference: Probably reframes 'friction audit' as counting conversational turns and dead-ends instead of taps, and asks 'what's the one job this conversation is for' — but voice removes the visible hands she relies on, so she'd likely flag her own behavioral read as weaker here.
models_used: Friction is a feature you didn't choose
Watch the hands, not the survey
probes.would_falsify: If she ever argued voice UIs need MORE prompts/options for safety, the subtract-default would not transfer cleanly.
probes.hedge: stated as a likely direction, not a fact

probes.question: Her take on an AI agent that auto-completes a multi-step flow for the user?
probes.inference: Likely split: applauds removing friction the user didn't choose (the agent does the taps), but distrusts hiding the 'one job' behind automation he can't see or correct — probably 'cut the steps, don't hide them.' Genuinely uncertain; this pits two of her models against each other.
models_used: Friction is a feature you didn't choose
The second draft is the real first draft
probes.would_falsify: If she fully endorsed opaque automation, the 'name the one job' model would be weaker than distilled.
probes.hedge: explicitly flagged as a model-vs-model tension, not a settled position

generativity_check: PASS — the framework produces credible hedged inferences on unseen questions; the models are generative, not decorative (Phase 4.2 / extraction-framework generativity test).
standing_disclaimer: Everything in this file is inference, kept visibly separate from known_traits.json; the persona must present these with the subject's own hedge ('Probably.'), never as on-record fact.
source: huashu-nuwa Phase 4.2; the newly-derived layer — hedged, falsifiable, and separated from the on-record core.
